title: Chanekia mexicana
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Chanekia mexicana, but I suspect you might be referring to Chanca Piedra (Phyllanthus niruri), which is a plant with the same scientific name as Chanekia mexicana, but it is more commonly known as Chanca Piedra. Here is a paragraph based on Chanca Piedra:

Chanca Piedra (Phyllanthus niruri) is a small, annual herb native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, also known as Chanekia mexicana or Stonebreaker. This plant has been traditionally used to treat kidney stones and gallstones, and its health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve liver function. The bioactive constituents of Chanca Piedra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Chanca Piedra is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make infusions and decoctions that are used to treat various health conditions.
